cross_scene_arcs.title: From Isolation to Connection
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Helena transitions from emotional withdrawal and isolation in the attic to forming a deep, intimate bond with Berlin, culminating in a tender farewell that signifies both connection and loss.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: To remain emotionally protected within the confines of the attic and her memories.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She transitions into solitude as Berlin leaves, marking the end of their companionship and the beginning of her emotional isolation.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The future of their relationship remains uncertain, with no clear path forward after Berlin's departure.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Confronting the Past, Unlocking the Truth
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Helena moves from suppressing traumatic memories to actively confronting the truth of Amber's death and participating in the investigation, ultimately choosing loyalty to Berlin over external obligations.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: To avoid confronting the truth of Amber's death and suppress traumatic memories.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: HELENA aligns more closely with Berlin, defying external pressure (Mr. Goodridge) at his behest, deepening their bond.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The full implications of the name 'John' and whether HELENA will fully engage with the investigation remain uncertain.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Erosion of Safety
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Helena's sense of security progressively unravels as subtle signs of intrusion escalate into direct physical threat, revealing her as a target of a stalker and culminating in a violent confrontation.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: To maintain safety and normalcy in her private space.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She is immobilized by terror after the intruder reveals himself, forces a knife into her hand, and threatens her with violence, marking a complete loss of control and safety.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The intruder's identity, his connection to 'Jenny' and the referenced murdered friend, and his intentions toward HELENA remain unclear, creating intense psychological and physical uncertainty.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Love, Loss, and Emotional Resilience
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Helena experiences the rise and collapse of a profound romantic connection with Berlin—from hopeful intimacy to painful abandonment—revealing her emotional depth and resilience amid unmet vulnerability.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: She seeks to prolong or soften the moment of separation, clinging to their connection.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Helena remains unaware but is now symbolically and physically left behind, marking a rupture in her relationship with Berlin.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The emotional consequences of Berlin's departure and the safety of Helena are left uncertain.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Performance and Identity Under Pressure
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Helena navigates shifting roles—from hidden observer to social performer to vulnerable lover—struggling to maintain agency over her identity amid external expectations and emotional upheaval.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Helena begins the physical process of returning to Berlin's house, signaling renewed involvement in their shared life.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Helena's deeper emotional pain remains unarticulated and misunderstood by Berlin, suggesting ongoing disconnection beneath physical reconciliation.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Erosion of Authority and Rise of Conflict with Berlin
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: ROSS's authority is progressively challenged by Berlin's independent investigation, leading to an escalating conflict rooted in institutional control versus truth-seeking. What begins as jurisdictional tension evolves into active obstruction and personal antagonism.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: ROSS arrives at the crime scene expecting full control, only to find Berlin already investigating.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: ROSS openly challenges Berlin's conclusions, but fails to shift the investigation's direction, leaving their relationship fractured.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The power struggle between institutional loyalty and investigative integrity remains unresolved, with ROSS ultimately marginalized.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Moral and Emotional Descent Amid Institutional Corruption
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: ROSS becomes increasingly entangled in a web of suppressed truths and personal disillusionment, revealing his complicity in covering up past cases like 'Jennifer' while grappling with emotional collapse and professional doubt.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: ROSS suppresses Berlin's inquiry into the frozen hand, signaling alignment with institutional secrecy.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: ROSS delivers a drunken monologue exposing his burnout and moral fatigue, revealing deep personal scars from years of compromised integrity.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: ROSS's true motivations for protecting the status quo—fear, loyalty, or guilt—remain unspoken, leaving his moral accountability ambiguous.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Fractured Partnership and Shifting Mentorship
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: ROSS's relationship with Berlin evolves from mentorship and camaraderie to antagonism and failed intervention, reflecting both a personal connection and a generational clash in policing philosophy.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: ROSS shares a personal story to encourage Berlin to quit smoking, establishing emotional rapport.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: ROSS attempts to warn Berlin against self-destruction, but is rebuffed, marking the breakdown of their mentor-mentee dynamic.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether Berlin will internalize any of ROSS's warnings or repeat his path of obsession remains uncertain.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Deteriorating Physical and Existential Resilience
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: ROSS's physical and mental state progressively deteriorates from determined investigator to vulnerable, isolated figure, culminating in his violent death that symbolizes the cost of relentless duty.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: ROSS pushes through exhaustion at the garbage dump, searching for evidence despite physical strain.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: ROSS is shot in the throat, unable to speak or act, marking the complete loss of agency and voice.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: ROSS's final attempt to speak suggests uncommunicated truth or regret, leaving emotional and narrative closure incomplete.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Tension Between Duty and Domestic Life
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: ROSS's fleeting interactions with home life highlight the growing rift between his professional obsession and familial responsibilities, underscoring the personal cost of his commitment to justice.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: ROSS cuts short a visit home, ignoring Margie's quiet disappointment, reaffirming duty over family.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: ROSS is seen supporting Berlin from his home, now fully immersed in mission life, with no further engagement with domestic space.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The emotional toll on ROSS's marriage and whether reconciliation was ever possible remains unexplored.
